product_title: Plum Smooth Sweet Plum Soft Wax 16 oz
clean_review_text: I had a leg and bikini wax with this product and liked it a great deal. In the past, I've used soy wax (Nufree) in salons and this is also a great product because it doesn't bond to the skin making it much less painful and more to the point doesn't cause near the amount of skin irritation. The Plum Smooth wax does the same thing but with the added bonus of having a more pleasant smell. I also found that I had even less post-wax irritation with this than with any other wax I've used. For my at-home waxing I've used consumer available wax strips like Veet, etc. but these universally are low quality and, in addition to being more painful (even to an experienced waxer like me), cause irritation and sometimes even bruising/broken skin in the more sensitive areas where the skin it thinner. This product is better than anything else I've tried and here's hoping that the results continue to be as good as the first couple times of use. As a side note - the other reviewer had negative results and correctly stated that what works for some people may not work with others - in my experience people that have not had good results with soy wax or the Plum Smooth due to irritation it has been because of allergies or incorrect use of the product, but these are generally considered to be much gentler on the skin than any sugar waxes or products you can purchase as a consumer.

product_title: Regal Ware Portable Induction Cooktop
clean_review_text: I bought the induction cooktop while it was on sale, just to try it out (after seeing demos on Cook's Illustrated). The biggest concern with all induction cooktops is that you must have the right cookware, but here I wanted to focus on what may be unique to this cooktop (versus others you might get). PROS: Very fast at bringing pots and pans to temperature (for example, I can boil water in about 2 minutes using this cooktop, and it takes about 5 minutes on my electric stove), and fast at temperature control. There are also some nice safety features. CONS: This does not have a continuous temp control, but 6 temps- I found that many times I need a slow simmer, but one temp was too high, and the next one down was too low. Another issue with low temps is that there is not a continuous low temp- The temperature pulses on and off. One annoyance (common or not?) is that when you lift a pan off the cooktop, it automatically gives a warning and shuts off. Conclusions for me: Nice for occasional use, but not a complete replacement for electric or gas.

product_title: Upgraded Dryer Repair Kit dryer roller kit for LG Kenmore Dryers Includes 4581EL2002C Dryer Drum Roller Assembly 4400EL2001A Dryer Belt and 4561EL3002A Dryer Idler Pulley, Pictures 6, 7 are Fit Models
clean_review_title: Fully restored my LG Steam dryer
clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:183e6f4b86bf51e723b3b8b53b9c63ba]] My wife and I love our LG Steam Dryer and Washer units. Last week the dryer started making a really bad screeching sound and I knew immediately that the drum rollers were going out. I ordered the Dryer Repair Kit and it came the very next day. The hardest part of the repair was getting to the back of my dryer since our laundry room is more of an overgrown closet than an actual room. At any rate I managed to get the 3 screws off the back of the dryer by reaching OVER the dryer while standing on a step stool. Once the 3 screws come off, the top of the dryer slides off towards the rear. The rest of the dryer comes apart in a fairly straightforward manner. I took a lot of Pix with my Iphone just to make sure it went back together correctly. Once the control panel came off and the door assembly was removed, it was easy to remove the rest of the front panel supports and the drum itself. I was then able to rotate the remaining base of the dryer since it was much lighter, to get to the rear screws that hold on the mounting plate that holds the 2 rear drum wheels. Removed all the wheels and replaced them with the wheels in the kit. Next replaced the idler pulley, and the drum with the new belt. Re-attached the control panel, front door etc. Started the dryer and it now runs perfectly! Saved a ton of money in the process.

product_title: Avanti WDB101, Color
clean_review_text: The assembly instructions leave out a couple of important things. First, there is no part “P”. There are two rods labeled P but these are actually the other two E parts that you screw together to form the legs. Now about those E parts: two of them have grooves in the middle and two don’t. The ones with the grooves in the middle go on the back legs. The grooves are what hold the plastic gizmos that hold the brace in place. This is not mentioned in the instructions. Once assembled, the thing does not seem very sturdy. The wire that forms the shelf for the dryer bows under the weight of the dryer which is about 50 lbs. I am going to get a board to put under the dryer to make it a bit more stable. So given the poor quality and the pain in the neck it was to assemble, I wouldn’t recommend this unit.

clean_review_title: A fraction of the repair cost compared to calling an appliance guy!
clean_review_text: We have an old Caloric brand wall oven that came with the house. I have no idea how old it is, but within a year or two of living here, the oven stopped working. It would glow, but no gas would come out and it would never start. I called an appliance repair man who determined it was a bad ignitor. He explained to me that the gas won't turn on until the ignitor reaches a certain temp, so either the ignitor wasn't getting hot enough, or it wasn't sending the signal to turn on the gas when it did. Either way, the problem was entirely with the ignitor. He replaced it, and something like $200 later, the oven was back to working. Until it stopped working again about a year after that. This time it didn't even glow, the oven would just do nothing. Instead of another expensive repair, I decided on a hunch to assume the part the repair man brought was lower quality than the original. I looked it up online and found a match here on Amazon. I felt confident that I could swap it myself now that I saw the repair guy do it, and sure enough this did the trick for a fraction of what I would pay him to come back! Its been a little over a month since putting it in, and so far so good. I'm hoping this will last longer than the last one, but even if it doesn't, worst case scenario is that I'll spend $20-30 on an annual repair until we can afford to replace the whole oven! I can deal with that.

product_title: Gevi Household Ice Maker Machine | Countertop Icemaker with Self Cleaning Function | 9 Pcs in 6-8 Mins with 2 Optional Ice Sizes | Making Max 26Lbs/Day | Ideal for Home Party Kitchen RV Camping (Blue)
clean_review_title: Made in China. Works well as long as you have proper expectations for the machine.
clean_review_text: There are quite a few of these icemakers being sold by assorted Chinese companies on Amazon right now. They are all virtually the same thing, though some of them look a little bit different. As long as your expectations meet what the icemaker can do, then it’s fine. But if your expectations exceed what the icemaker can do, then you will be disappointed. Things to keep in mind… First of all, this icemaker does not keep the ice frozen. The ice goes into a basket and then, if it is not used, it will melt and the water will fall to the bottom water reservoir and then it will be refrozen again and again through the machine. The only way to keep the ice frozen is if you remove it from the basket and place the ice into a freezer. But when you do that, the ice will be “wet” so it will freeze together and you will need to “bang” it apart. Second… To say that this icemaker will make 26 pounds of ice each day is a bit of a misnomer. Because the only way for it to do that is if you continuously remove the ice from the basket each time the basket is full. It takes about an hour for the basket to fill up completely. So you would have to have someone removing the ice… Day and night… For it to make 26 pounds of ice. Of course you will have to continually fill the reservoir with water as well. So unless you are working in a factory or elsewhere that someone is there twenty-four hours a day, then you will not get 26 pounds of ice. You should stop the icemaker from making ice when you are not using it. For example, in the evenings when you go to bed. Otherwise the ice will just continually melt and be recycled over and over and over. This icemaker is made in China and being sold by Chinese company on Amazon. At the current price of 100 bucks it’s certainly twice the price it should be, but overall it is fine… Again… As long as you have the right expectations.

product_title: Fidget Spin Ring: Möbii Wearable, Stainless Steel Ring: Loose Rings Möbius Moving Rolling Fidget Design
clean_review_text: To begin, I have purchased each of the following (many in multiples): Möbii Daydream, Deep Thought, Half Thought, Medium Möbii, Medium Stainless Steel Möbii, Small Möbii, Small Stainless Steel and Copper Möbii, Anodized Aluminum Spin Ring, Stainless Steel Spin Ring. (All except the Small Stainless Steel/Copper Möbii and the Medium Stainless Steel Möbii are pictured above basically in the order they are listed. The two not pictured are the same size as the other Small and Medium Möbii and are well represented with the seller’s pictures. And, please ignore the busted knuckle from yard work. :) ) If you are like me, and like to comparison shop using reviews, I hope the inclusion of each item is helpful. That said, for simplicity, I will be copying and pasting the same review on each of the items I have purchased. While the Daydream is a bit large for my hand, it works because it is so lightweight. The individual links/rings are thicker than the Medium and Small Möbii, and I like the feel and movement. The Deep Thought is nice and heavy with a good feel to it. I purchased it as a gift to my husband. It fits well in his large hands, and he seems to like it. The Half Thought is wonderful! It is definitely one of my favorites. It has wonderfully simple and smooth unending movement. The weight is just right. It does make more noise than the tighter fitting Möbii fidgets—I mean, they’re stainless steel rings. The noise doesn’t bother me, and it’s basically as loud as you make it. The Small Möbii fidgets are really nice for discreet fidgeting at meetings, etc., and they work well for just fingertip fidgeting. The colors are vibrant, and the customization option is a lot of fun! The Small Stainless Steel and Copper Möbii is really nice—another favorite! The weight is great, and the movement is very smooth. I really like the copper mixed with the stainless steel. The Medium Möbii fidgets probably get the most use (yes, another favorite) because of the perfect size, nice movement, and fun colors. They are lightweight and quiet (not silent). I do also have a Medium Stainless Steel Möbii, which, of course, has a little more weight to it and a good feel. The Stainless Steel version probably has slightly better overall movement. Although, I do still tend to pick up the brightly colored Aluminum version more often. The Spin Rings (Anodized Aluminum and Stainless Steel) are both wonderful, whether you wear them at the base of the finger or in the middle—depending on fidget preferences. Of course, the Stainless Steel version is heavier. I really like both. I like more rings per spin ring for fidgeting and spinning, but obviously, more rings make the entire ring much thicker. If I wear a thick ring on my right (dominant) hand, it tends to interfere with writing. I usually just switch hands or set it aside. Everything I’ve received has been very well made and delivered in a cute little Möbii-branded bag. As other reviewers have stated, the personal, hand-written “thank you” note is a nice touch. I had a few large orders, and the seller even included a free Small Möbii in two of the shipments!! I was super excited! (Geoff, I hope you’re reading this: Thank you so much!!!) These make great gifts and have been a hit with my family and friends. I am addicted! I highly recommend any of these Steel Lynx products, y’all!! You can’t go wrong!

I love meal replacement drinks over plain protein as the added vitamins, minerals, and fiber make me feel better than protein alone. I make smoothies and use prefab nutrition drinks like this as a means to get something in my body when I dont feel like eating. I often dont have an appetite until half way through my day, but I will feel some low level hunger, and I know I need something to power me through my physical work day. So a simple smoothie usually works (and allows me to add in other supplements such as collagen, mushroom powder). That being said, at some point I am never quite satisfied with a liquid meal, but still dont want a meal. So I usually add in a few nibbles of something, whether it be fruit, veg, cheese stick, snack bar, etc. Works great as a snack, or even as a "dessert" Sweetened primarily with allulose, and finishes with some sucralose (Splenda!). I dont really have an opinion on either sweetener. I find both are very sweet, without an aftertaste, and I dont have digestive issues from it. Pack of six, 32 ounce bottles which contain 4 (8 ounce) servings (all told same as a pack of 24 bottles at 8 ounces each). I like the option of it having a few servings worth in one big bottle as it cuts down on packaging. But it also reduces my ability to just grab and go. However, they do carry these in other sizes! Price per ounce is very similar to others with blood glucose support in mind. I have to say, I didnt know what to expect. With my first sip, I was impressed with taste, texture, and how there was no odd taste or funny after taste. Really quite pleasing flavor. The extra thick texture makes it versatile. Thick makes it feel like a shake, and triggers belly into thinking there is something in there. But it also allows for it to be watered down some if you want something to sip. I'm impressed, and would recommend this. Of course, take a look at ingredients and decide if it is good for your needs.
